text: You were celebrating too early and you fell down the hole. You were down to your last life and it was a Game Over, and so instead of starting the level over, you now have to start the entire World over (unless if you are using save states on an NES emulator and you can try again from the last saved point with the lives you had left prior to game overing). You get so pissed off that you hit your NES, the game froze and now have to start the entire game over from scratch.

text: It's funny how you can die at the end of certain games. In Super Mario Bros. 2, even after you defeat Wart, you have to wait until he dies and disappears from the scene, as his body can still hurt you. And in Super Mario Galaxy 2, there's a crazy glitch that can cause you to die DURING the credits! (as you can still control Mario during them).

text:
Ugh. Yeah. That figures. Disappointing. I had hope yet for Japan's sense of humor, but I guess nothing as usual.
Yeah, be it anime, or English localizations, our fellow Statesmen are always improving on the English translations of Japanese products.
author: @Fudgaboutit

text: You know, he could actually have saved that fall with the raccoon tail float if he reacted quick enough.
author: @Fudgaboutit

text: I don't fucking believe that at all, and I would be shocked as hell to find that was true. There's no way there can be something from that game after all these years that I haven't seen. I plumbed (no pun intended) the dephs of that game inside and out, and did everything. I know EVERYTHING. I know when and where to jump at every moment. I would have found that out if it was true. The only part of the game I don't really know is that last sub-castle in World 8, which I always skipped.
author: @Fudgaboutit

text: That, "I'm sorry Mario, but the princess is in another castle. Just kidding. Ha ha ha." Might be the funniest thing the Japanese have ever done in their history. That was such a cool and hilarious joke. Were they even aware of the meme status that line had from the first Super Mario Bros? But it's just such a funny joke, and so cool that they would be keen enough to put that in the much-hyped Super Mario Bros. 3, a major commercial game. I remember nearly shitting a brick when I first read it
author: @Fudgaboutit

text:
Imagine if you are a kid in the﻿ year 1990, and this is you playing Bowser for the first time after countless days/hours/weeks of playing the game, after endless hours of chattering with your friends(who haven't made it this far) about how far you have gotten, while sporting your Reebok Pumps with the orange basketball on the tongue of the shoe that your grandma paid $129.99 for..
 
and this happens. This is what gave me stomach ulcers as a child. Nintendo was larger than life back in the day.
